description: Fictional public example for Academy source governance. Live acquisition remains proof-gated.

github_repository.authorization_required: public or organization-approved repository access and license review
github_repository.deletion_policy: track commit/tag and repository archived/deleted state
github_repository.label: GitHub repositories and systems practice
github_repository.live_proof_boundary: PG-HERMES workspace proof for applied repo-derived training
github_repository.permission_policy: license and selected-file manifest required before copying code or docs
github_repository.raw_storage_policy: raw files only when license and product policy allow; derived patterns preferred

organization_private.authorization_required: Captain/operator supplied material and private data governance approval
organization_private.deletion_policy: honor owner deletion, retention, and audience-scope changes before updates
organization_private.label: Organization-provided private material
organization_private.live_proof_boundary: policy decision plus PG-HERMES workspace proof
organization_private.permission_policy: organization policy decides reuse, retention, and Agent audience boundaries
organization_private.raw_storage_policy: private raw storage only with explicit operator policy and scoped access

reddit_discussion.authorization_required: official API/OAuth access and platform retention policy
reddit_discussion.deletion_policy: deleted or removed content must be tombstoned before Agent update
reddit_discussion.label: Reddit and practitioner discussion
reddit_discussion.live_proof_boundary: policy decision plus PG-PROVIDER/PG-BOTS proof where used
reddit_discussion.permission_policy: raw user content is restricted; derived non-identifying lessons require policy approval
reddit_discussion.raw_storage_policy: never in the unattended local slice

scholarly_standard.authorization_required: open-access, standards-body, or operator-approved full text rights
scholarly_standard.deletion_policy: track superseded, retracted, contradicted, and replaced documents
scholarly_standard.label: Scholarly, standards, and whitepapers
scholarly_standard.live_proof_boundary: PG-PROVIDER for provider-assisted synthesis from bounded source metadata
scholarly_standard.permission_policy: metadata/abstract allowed when full text is not licensed for storage
scholarly_standard.raw_storage_policy: metadata-only by default; raw full text only with open-access or approved rights

skill_tool_catalog.authorization_required: local, bundled, organization, or reviewed public skill source
skill_tool_catalog.deletion_policy: remove or quarantine unapproved/superseded skills before Agent updates
skill_tool_catalog.label: Skills, MCP servers, tools, and templates
skill_tool_catalog.live_proof_boundary: PG-HERMES for trained workspace behavior after skills are staged
skill_tool_catalog.permission_policy: public skills must be reviewed before they influence an Agent
skill_tool_catalog.raw_storage_policy: metadata-only until skill review accepts code/content storage

video_transcript.authorization_required: creator, platform, Captain, or operator transcript authorization
video_transcript.deletion_policy: tombstone transcript snapshots when rights are revoked or source disappears
video_transcript.label: Video and transcript
video_transcript.live_proof_boundary: policy decision plus PG-PROVIDER transcription proof
video_transcript.permission_policy: transcripts require explicit access rights; ASR requires separate approval
video_transcript.raw_storage_policy: raw transcript only with recorded authorization; derived summaries preferred

web_article.authorization_required: stable public URL, authorship/date, and allowed snapshot policy
web_article.deletion_policy: mark removed pages as removed; archive only when policy allows
web_article.label: Web articles, docs, blogs, newsletters, and threads
web_article.live_proof_boundary: PG-PROVIDER for live search/acquisition and generated synthesis
web_article.permission_policy: store metadata and derived lesson cards unless snapshot rights are explicit
web_article.raw_storage_policy: derived summaries by default; raw snapshots require policy approval

wikimedia.authorization_required: public API use with revision and license metadata
wikimedia.deletion_policy: track revision and supersession; keep citation to the exact revision when possible
wikimedia.label: Wikipedia and Wikimedia
wikimedia.live_proof_boundary: PG-PROVIDER for live generation, local fixtures only here
wikimedia.permission_policy: CC/license metadata required; use as map and bibliography, not sole authority
wikimedia.raw_storage_policy: allowed with compatible license, but derived summaries preferred

local_fixture_shape.notes: Local fake acquisition fixtures are test artifacts only. They must preserve no_network/no_write and include each lane's required metadata before they can become AcademySource records.
